paper.title_en: Modelling of the magnetic cross-talk effect of the HEPS storage ring
paper.abstract_en: The High Energy Photon Source (HEPS) is a fourth-generation synchrotron light source located in Beijing, China. To achieve ultra-low emittance, a compact hybrid seven-bend achromat lattice design has been adopted for the storage ring, together with high-gradient quadrupoles, combined-function magnets and longitudinal gradient dipoles. Magnetic cross-talk can degrade machine performance and therefore must be carefully evaluated. To this end, several methods based on one-dimensional and three-dimensional magnetic fields data are used to model the cross-talk effect of magnets in the HEPS storage ring. This paper introduces the detailed modelling approaches and investigates the impact of cross-talk on the linear optics of the HEPS storage ring.
